title: peplis nummulariifolia subsp. hispidula
intro: Peplis nummulariifolia subsp. hispidula, also known as the hispid hairy peplis, is a subspecies of the Peplis nummulariifolia plant, a member of the waterwort family (Haloragaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Peplis nummulariifolia subsp. hispidula include the potential treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a diuretic to aid in the removal of excess fluids from the body. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are believed to offer relief from various health conditions.
